included: stage open through nanousdapi and NANOUSD_BACKEND
C API prim traversal through NanousdBackend_v1
diagnostic collection through the backend vtable
excluded: nanousd core-only phase probes that bypass nanousdapi
full value-resolution, interpolation, clip, and xform benchmarks

model.backend_comparison: The primary signal is comparative behavior across backends running the same nanousd C API benchmark and fixture set.
model.baseline_policy: Baselines are selected by benchmark invocation. Common baselines are nanousd default backend and OpenUSD backend when available.
model.gating_policy: The generated backend must be able to complete the supported fixture runs. Concrete generated-vs-baseline timing ratios remain optional until separate performance contracts set machine-independent expectations.
model.correctness_gate: A backend performance result is not meaningful unless the corresponding C API behavior passes the relevant correctness contracts for the fixture shape.

stage_open.expected_direction: The generated read-only backend should converge toward nanousd default backend open cost for supported USDA fixtures.

traversal.expected_direction: Traversal should scale with populated prim and property counts, not with reparsing or full-stage scans per query.

memory.expected_direction: Retained memory should reflect target-native stage, path, token, and value storage rather than adapter JSON duplication.

comparison_signals: before/after generated backend ratios for the same fixture
contract_pressure: slow open points at layer-open, composition, and stage-population contracts
slow traversal points at stage indexing and handle lifetime contracts
slow attribute counts point at document-model property storage contracts
high RSS points at value, path, token, or adapter JSON retention contracts
